name: minecraft-java-1.21.11-any%_fastest_personalized
description: Best possible evidence-based strategy to beat Minecraft Java Edition 1.21.11 on Normal without keepInventory, optimized for fastest completion and tailored to the user skill level.

paths.name: root-route-comparison
paths.description: Coarse expected-completion-time comparison for top-level route families: seed-assisted standard, practice-gated, blind standard.

paths.name: seed-assisted-vs-practice-vs-blind-model
paths.description: Coarse expected completion time model for the three root families.

routes.name: Seed-Assisted Standard Route
routes.theme: Fastest practical full Minecraft completion for a first-time Normal player
routes.summary: Use legal external seed scouting / seedmap to pick a strong world with village, ruined portal, and good Nether access; then execute a conventional Overworld -> Nether -> End completion line with minimal blind search.
routes.decisionReason: Best root family: it removes the largest uncontrolled variance by using legal seed scouting while staying within a standard, practical survival completion line.
routes.evidence: Official Minecraft speedrun guidance recommends resetting for village starts and emphasizes practice; seed scouting is allowed by project constraints; coarse root model favors low-variance preplanned starts for a first-time player.
routes.generationNote: Depth-2 seed-assisted world-selection split generated: portal-first, fortress-proximate, village-food-first, bastion-accelerated, stronghold-proximate.

routes.name: Practice-Gated Route
routes.theme: Fastest practical full Minecraft completion for a first-time Normal player
routes.summary: Spend a short calibration block on targeted skill tests and practice maps for the user's weakest bottlenecks, then commit to the fastest completion line they can execute reliably.
routes.decisionReason: Useful because the user is an inexperienced full-clear player and explicitly values skill tests; targeted practice can prevent costly failures.
routes.evidence: Official Minecraft article stresses practice; practice maps exist for portals, bastions, fortress, crafting, overworld movement, and endfights; user preference favors caution and test-backed routes.

routes.name: Blind Standard Route
routes.theme: Fastest practical full Minecraft completion for a first-time Normal player
routes.summary: Do not scout or preselect a seed; start from a random world and complete the game using standard survival progression and on-the-fly adaptation.
routes.decisionReason: Lowest-priority root family because blind spawning adds the most variance and gives no skill calibration benefit for a cautious first-time clearer.
routes.evidence: Blind routing leaves early structure search to luck; seed-assisted route and practice-gated route both reduce expected failure/time more directly.

routes.name: Fortress-Proximate Nether Seed
routes.summary: Prioritize seeds whose Nether side quickly reaches a fortress, reducing blaze-hunt time after entry.
routes.note: Broad seed filter centered on the most time-sensitive Nether structure.
routes.decisionReason: Keeps because fortress proximity directly shortens blaze acquisition, the main midgame bottleneck for a standard End route.

routes.name: Bastion-Accelerated Seed
routes.summary: Prioritize seeds that make piglin trading and Bastion loot a major acceleration path for pearls, gold, and backup gear.
routes.note: Higher-payoff but more execution-heavy structure stack.

routes.name: Ruined-Portal-First Seed
routes.summary: Prioritize seeds where a ruined portal or near-complete Nether entry is effectively the opening move, minimizing early mining and portal setup.
routes.note: Broad seed filter centered on immediate Nether access.

routes.name: Stronghold-Proximate Seed
routes.summary: Prioritize seeds that shorten the Overworld search phase by placing the Stronghold relatively close or easy to triangulate.
routes.note: Downstream endgame shortcut criterion rather than an opening utility criterion.

routes.name: Village-Food-First Seed
routes.summary: Prioritize seeds that guarantee a safe, resource-rich Overworld start: nearby village, food, beds, and low-friction first-night survival.
routes.note: Broad seed filter centered on early safety and low search variance.
